summary: Product AC evidence can now attach Architecture Apply context without letting architecture context prove the AC by itself. Tests show context-only evidence is downgraded, while real user-visible verification plus architecture apply context passes and renders both sources in report.

sources.label: npx vitest run test/core.test.js -t 'product evidence can reference architecture apply context|architecture apply records do not count|evidence records flexible reviewable sources'
sources.command: npx vitest run test/core.test.js -t 'product evidence can reference architecture apply context|architecture apply records do not count|evidence records flexible reviewable sources'

sources.label: npx vitest run test/cli-commands.test.js -t 'evidence add command module records flexible reviewable sources'
sources.command: npx vitest run test/cli-commands.test.js -t 'evidence add command module records flexible reviewable sources'

reviewability: Run the listed tests; inspect report/status sources for architecture-apply role=context and command/product evidence side by side.
limitations: This verifies evidence semantics and reporting; it does not prove a real external project flow until dogfood.
